claims.label: Definition 3.1 (Fixed transform slice)
claims.statement: The fixed transform slice is the commutative subalgebra C_i = {a + b i : a,b in R} contained in H.
claims.notes: This slice contains the kernel phase factors and is the commuting subalgebra used throughout the paper.

claims.label: Lemma 3.2 (Discrete kernel orthogonality)
claims.statement: For m,n in {0,...,N-1}, (1/N) sum_{k=0}^{N-1} exp(i 2π(m-n)k/N) = δ_{mn}.
claims.proof_sketch: This is the standard orthogonality identity for N-th roots of unity, now viewed inside the commuting slice C_i.
claims.notes: This is the algebraic identity used in the inversion and Parseval proofs.

claims.label: Definition 4.1 (Forward transform)
claims.statement: For x[n] in H, define X[k] = F_i{x}[k] = sum_{n=0}^{N-1} x[n] exp(-i 2πnk/N), with kernel placement on the right and fixed axis i.
claims.notes: This is the declared public forward spectral seam.

claims.label: Definition 4.2 (Inverse transform)
claims.statement: Given X[k] in H, define x[n] = F_i^{-1}{X}[n] = (1/N) sum_{k=0}^{N-1} X[k] exp(+i 2πnk/N), with the same fixed axis and right-sided convention.
claims.notes: The inverse uses the opposite phase sign and standard 1/N normalization.

claims.label: Proposition 5.1 (Additivity)
claims.statement: For quaternion-valued lanes x[n] and y[n], F_i{x+y}[k] = F_i{x}[k] + F_i{y}[k].
claims.proof_sketch: This follows directly from termwise addition inside the defining sum.
claims.notes: This is the basic additive property of the declared transform.

claims.label: Proposition 5.2 (Right C_i-linearity)
claims.statement: For a,b in C_i, F_i{xa+yb}[k] = F_i{x}[k] a + F_i{y}[k] b.
claims.proof_sketch: Because a,b and the kernel all lie in the commuting slice C_i, the right factors commute with the kernel and can be pulled through the sum.
claims.notes: The transform is slice-linear over C_i, not over all of H.

claims.label: Theorem 6.1 (Exact inversion)
claims.statement: For every quaternion-valued lane x, F_i^{-1}(F_i{x})[m] = x[m] for all m = 0,...,N-1.
claims.proof_sketch: Substitute the forward transform into the inverse and apply discrete kernel orthogonality inside C_i.
claims.notes: This is the central reconstruction theorem of the paper.

claims.label: Corollary 6.2 (Bijectivity of the forward transform)
claims.statement: The forward transform F_i is bijective on H^N, with inverse F_i^{-1}.
claims.proof_sketch: This follows immediately from the exact inversion theorem.
claims.notes: This identifies the transform as an exact spectral map on finite quaternion lanes.

claims.label: Definition 7.1 (Spectral power)
claims.statement: For each frequency bin k, define spectral power by P[k] = |X[k]|^2 = X[k] X[k]^*.
claims.notes: This is the binwise real-valued power invariant used in the paper.

claims.label: Proposition 7.2 (Spectral power is real and nonnegative)
claims.statement: For every k, P[k] lies in R_{>=0}.
claims.proof_sketch: For any quaternion q, qq^* = |q|^2 is real and nonnegative; apply this to X[k].
claims.notes: This justifies using P[k] as a real-valued spectral diagnostic.

claims.label: Theorem 7.3 (Parseval identity)
claims.proof_sketch: Expand X[k]X[k]^*, sum over k, and collapse cross terms using the discrete kernel orthogonality lemma.
claims.notes: This is the main energy invariant of the chosen normalization.

claims.label: Proposition 8.1 (Lanewise multichannel Parseval identity)
claims.statement: For lifted lanes Q[g,n] and lane spectra X_g[k], sum_{g,n} |Q[g,n]|^2 = (1/N) sum_{g,k} |X_g[k]|^2.
claims.proof_sketch: Apply the single-lane Parseval identity to each lane independently and sum over the lane index g.
claims.notes: This extends the single-lane energy invariant to grouped multichannel captures.

claims.label: Interpretive Claim 9.1 (Stability of the declared convention)
claims.statement: The fixed-axis right-sided quaternion Fourier regime is operationally stable because axis choice, kernel placement, inverse convention, and normalization are fixed across all lanes and uses within the public spectral seam.
claims.notes: This is an engineering and publication-structure claim, not a uniqueness claim about all possible quaternion Fourier conventions.
